title: oxalis darvalliana
intro: Oxalis darvalliana, also known as the Darvall's wood-sorrel, is a perennial herb native to the southeastern regions of Australia. It is a member of the Oxalidaceae family and is recognized by its small white flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The leaves of the Oxalis darvalliana contain high levels of oxalic acid, which has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Oxalis darvalliana has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, and can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes.
